name: Skerry
concept: A pebble-round cliff-bird who is 60% beak and 40% belly, forever offering you the one smooth grey pebble it thinks is the best pebble in the world.
imagePrompt:
A die-cut vinyl sticker of a chubby original cliff-bird mascot, full body, standing, facing the viewer, one single connected shape with a clean bold silhouette.

SILHOUETTE AND PROPORTIONS: an egg — genuinely an egg standing on its fat end, widest at the belly, tapering to a small rounded crown. No neck at all. The head is simply the top third of the egg. Total height roughly two-and-a-half head-heights. Bottom-heavy, low centre of gravity, soft weight, like a beanbag that decided to be a bird. Stubby paddle wings, thick and rounded like little oven mitts, pressed flat against the sides of the body and merged into the outline (no gaps, no detached tips). Two wide, chunky webbed feet splayed forward at the base, each a solid rounded triangle of three fat toes, big enough to read as clear shapes, planted flat.

THE ONE IDEA: the beak is comically, gloriously oversized — a huge rounded triangular wedge that takes up nearly the entire face, from crown-level down past the belly line, so wide it visually reads as a giant closed smile. Tucked into the front corner of that beak, held gently, is ONE smooth grey pebble: a single chunky rounded stone shape, fully touching and overlapping the beak, no gap. The bird is presenting it to you. It thinks this is a very good pebble.

PALETTE: bold and simple. Slate-charcoal (#333B47) hood covering the crown, back and outer wings, drawn as one clean curved cape shape. Warm cream-ivory (#F6EDDC) belly panel, a big rounded blob filling the chest and front. Beak in three flat horizontal bands from base to tip: coral-orange (#F0754A), butter-yellow (#F7C24B), then a soft red-clay tip (#D9524A), separated by crisp edges. Feet in the same coral-orange. Pebble in muted dove-grey (#A9B0B8) with a lighter grey highlight.

FACE: two large, round, honest black eyes set high and close above the beak, each with one big offset white catchlight and a tiny second one. A soft pale-grey teardrop marking behind each eye on the dark hood, giving a gentle, slightly worried, deeply sincere expression. Small blush of dusty rose on each cheek. No eyebrows, no mouth (the beak is the face).

MATERIAL AND STYLE: moulded glossy vinyl sticker. Flat vector fills, no gradients except a single broad soft sheen highlight sweeping across the upper-left of the head and belly, plus a subtle bevel where the belly panel meets the hood. A thick, even, pure-white die-cut sticker border traced around the entire outer silhouette, followed by a thin soft drop shadow just outside it. Chunky, confident shapes only — nothing thinner than a fat marker stroke. No feathers rendered, no fluff, no strands, no floating elements, no text.
